title: Create mobile frontline packet from WMS, shipment, and supplier work
given: A demo operator has WMS, Control Tower, or supplier frontline access.
The tenant has open WMS tasks, Control Tower exceptions, supplier onboarding tasks, and linked document/note evidence.
Some work items require live confirmation while others can be captured offline.
when: The operator selects Create frontline packet.
then: The assistant persists an AssistantFrontlinePacket row.
The packet includes a mobile queue, quick actions, evidence checklist, offline risks, and permission scope.
No WMS task is completed, no shipment exception is closed, no supplier task is updated, and no evidence record is changed automatically.

title: Queue reviewed quick actions before source-system updates
given: A frontline packet exists with quick actions and evidence gaps.
The operator has edit access to at least one frontline domain.
when: The operator adds an approval note.
then: An AssistantActionQueueItem is created for frontline quick-action review.
The packet status changes to REVIEW_QUEUED and stores the approval note.
The audit event states WMS tasks, shipment exceptions, supplier tasks, and evidence records were not mutated automatically.

title: Role-safe mobile workflow with offline reconciliation
given: A warehouse user has WMS access but no Control Tower edit access.
A shipment exception requires live delivery confirmation in a poor-network environment.
when: The user reviews the live mobile preview.
then: Quick actions are enabled only for roles with action grants.
The preview marks delivery confirmation as an offline risk with supervisor review mitigation.
The assistant does not claim the action is complete until approved and reconciled.
